rationale: The citing paper references Frederick, Lee, and Baskin (2014) only in grouped literature lists: once as part of general context-effects background and once to note a broader debate about attraction-effect robustness in the future-research section. It does not engage that paper's specific mechanisms, evidence, or boundary-condition arguments in hypothesis development, study design, or interpretation of results. The focal empirical and theoretical development centers on serotonin effects on choice deferral and the compromise effect, so this citation functions as peripheral background mention.
